summary: Strip CLAUDECODE env vars before spawning SDK subprocess to prevent nested session rejection
context: Agent sessions fail with exit code 1, 0 turns, 0 tokens when the harness is run from within Claude Code. The SDK spawns `claude` as a subprocess which inherits CLAUDECODE=1 from the parent environment, causing the CLI to refuse with 'Claude Code cannot be launched inside another Claude Code session'.
rationale: Always pass a cleaned env to the SDK with CLAUDECODE and CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT removed. This is set unconditionally (not just when those vars are present) so the harness works identically whether run from a terminal or from within Claude Code. The condition-specific env vars are merged on top of the clean base env.

alternatives.option: Unset CLAUDECODE at process start in the CLI entrypoint
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would affect the harness process itself, not just subprocesses. Also fragile if other env vars are added in future.

alternatives.option: Use the SDK's custom spawn function to strip env vars
alternatives.reason_rejected: More complex, same outcome. The env option is the intended mechanism.
